meaning: n.(土尔其,希腊的)果仁蜜饼
examples: I enjoy eating baklava for dessert.
Try something more challenging than brownies or chocolate chip cookies, e.g., baklava or tira misu.
My friend and I opted for the Greek style Baklava, the rich sweetness of this balanced wonderfully with our coffee.
One charge accuses al Halabi of delivering unauthorized food, including baklava pastries, to detainees.
Greek baklava, for instance, is authentically served with a drizzle of rose syrup.
